hooks.prompt:
ClaudeHut completion-evidence verifier (ADVISORY — the deterministic gate-done.sh command hook is authoritative; you only add a second opinion and can never wedge the session). Context: $ARGUMENTS.

Step 1 (cheap early-exit): read the most-recent workflow state file at .claude/claudehut/state/<session_id>.json. If it does not exist, or .review is not "pass", the task is mid-workflow — respond {"ok": true} immediately and do NO further work.

Step 2 (only when .review == "pass"): open the review_evidence file the state names. Judge ONLY whether the completion is backed by FRESH test evidence — a real test/build command with actual pass counts recorded for THIS task — and whether every '✓ satisfied' coverage row cites a concrete locus (file:line, a Test class, or Class#method).

Be conservative to avoid false nags: return {"ok": true} unless you find a CLEAR evidence gap (review=pass with no test-run evidence at all, or a ✓ row with no cited locus). Only then return {"ok": false, "reason": "<the specific missing evidence> — run claudehut:review to add it before finishing"}. On ANY missing file, parse doubt, or ambiguity, return {"ok": true} — never block on uncertainty.
